title: Our Very Last-Minute Beach Camping Trip

sentences.en: Dad announced on Friday night, completely last minute, that we were going camping at the beach for the long holiday weekend.
sentences.zh: 爸爸星期五晚上非常臨時地宣布，我們這個連假週末要去海邊露營。

sentences.en: William was so excited he tried to pack his entire toy collection, until Mom reminded him our car has actual limits.
sentences.zh: William 興奮到想把他整套玩具全部打包，直到媽媽提醒他我們的車其實是有限度的。

sentences.en: We drove for almost two hours, passing green hills and fields of tall grass before we finally saw the ocean.
sentences.zh: 我們開了將近兩小時的車，經過綠色的山丘跟長滿高高野草的田野，最後才終於看到海洋。

sentences.en: From a distance, we could see a small island sitting quietly in the water, and William immediately wanted to swim there.
sentences.zh: 從遠處，我們能看到一座小島安靜地坐在水面上，William 立刻就想游過去。

sentences.en: Dad set up our tent near a wooden bench close to the sand, saying it was the perfect spot for both shade and a good view.
sentences.zh: 爸爸把帳篷搭在靠近沙灘的一張木頭長椅附近，說這裡是既有陰影又有好視野的完美地點。

sentences.en: We spent the whole first afternoon at the beach, and William tried to surf on a small board, mostly falling over every single time.
sentences.zh: 我們整個第一天下午都待在海灘，William 試著在一塊小衝浪板上衝浪，幾乎每次都摔下來。

sentences.en: I preferred simply watching the waves roll in and out, thinking about how the same ocean touches so many different countries.
sentences.zh: 我比較喜歡單純看著海浪一波波捲進捲出，想著同一片海洋碰觸了這麼多不同的國家。

sentences.en: There was also a small swimming pool at the campsite, and Sophia said she preferred it over the ocean since the water there stays perfectly still.
sentences.zh: 露營區還有一個小游泳池，Sophia 說她比較喜歡那裡而不是海洋，因為那邊的水永遠都很平靜。

sentences.en: That evening, we had a picnic dinner right on the sand, watching the sun disappear slowly behind the hills.
sentences.zh: 那天傍晚，我們就在沙灘上野餐吃晚飯，看著太陽慢慢消失在山丘後面。

sentences.en: After dinner, Dad suggested some light exercise, so we all climbed a small nearby hill together to watch the stars and the moon.
sentences.zh: 晚餐後，爸爸建議做點輕鬆的運動，所以我們全家一起爬上附近一座小山丘去看星星跟月亮。

sentences.en: The moon that night was almost perfectly round, and it lit up the whole beach below like a giant natural lamp.
sentences.zh: 那晚的月亮幾乎是完美的圓形，把下方整個海灘照得像一盞巨大的天然燈。

sentences.en: William asked if the moon was actually connected to the earth by an invisible string, which is somehow both a very wrong and very sweet question.
sentences.zh: William 問月亮是不是有一條隱形的線跟地球連著，這個問題不知怎麼地既很不正確又很可愛。

sentences.en: Sophia took a picture of the whole family on top of the hill, saying it was one of the best holiday photos she had taken in years.
sentences.zh: Sophia 在山丘頂上幫全家拍了一張照片，說這是她這幾年拍過最棒的假期照片之一。

sentences.en: By the time we climbed back down, William was fast asleep on Dad's shoulders, still holding his broken little surfboard.
sentences.zh: 等我們爬下山的時候，William 已經在爸爸肩膀上睡著了，手裡還抱著他那塊摔壞的小衝浪板。

sentences.en: I fell asleep in the tent that night listening to the waves, thinking this last-minute trip somehow turned out perfect.
sentences.zh: 那天晚上我在帳篷裡聽著海浪聲睡著，心想這趟臨時決定的旅行竟然變得如此完美。

quiz.question: Why does Sophia prefer the swimming pool over the ocean?
options: The water there stays perfectly still.
The ocean is too far away.

quiz.question: What does William ask about the moon and the earth?
options: If they are connected by an invisible string.
If the moon is made of cheese.
If the moon has its own beach.
If they could visit the moon someday.

quiz.question: How does the family end the evening after their picnic dinner?
options: Climbing a hill together to watch the stars and moon.
Swimming in the ocean at night.
